rationale: The cited 2002 JMKR paper is directly about ideation originality and shows that external examples can induce fixation and reduce originality, which conceptually overlaps with the citing paper’s core mechanism that exposure to neighbors’ ideas increases redundancy and lowers innovativeness. Given this close construct-level fit (new product ideation, originality/innovativeness, influence of others’ ideas), this is not clearly an incidental background mention and warrants full relation judgment.
